visual_attributes.morphology: robust, rounded body with a high, rounded forehead and no distinct beak
distinctive_features: small, rounded dorsal fin located two-thirds along the back
U-shaped blowhole on the left of the midline
visual_attributes.size_and_scale: 2.3 to 2.75 meters in length, weighing 90 to 200 kg
visual_attributes.structural_arrangement: rounded head with a blunt snout, small dorsal fin, and broad flippers

relationships.description: Closely resembles and shares the same genus, Orcaella

relationships.target: Bay of Bengal and Southeast Asia
relationships.description: Found in scattered subpopulations in these regions

contextual_attributes.origin: Bay of Bengal and Southeast Asia
contextual_attributes.historical_period: Described in 1866 by Sir Richard Owen
contextual_attributes.geographic_range: Bay of Bengal to New Guinea and the Philippines
contextual_attributes.cultural_significance: Important for local fisheries and ecosystems
